name: PM-RAHAT Scheme (Cashless Treatment)
description: Free, cashless emergency treatment for every road accident victim during the critical 'golden hour', regardless of who was at fault.
eligibility: Applies to every road accident victim, regardless of fault
Treatment must be sought at a hospital empanelled under the scheme
benefits: Up to ₹1.5 lakh of free treatment per victim
Covers up to 7 days of treatment from the date of the accident
No payment needed at the time of treatment; police verification happens separately and is not the victim's responsibility
process: Call 112 and ask for the nearest designated/empanelled hospital under the scheme
Visit the nearest empanelled hospital for treatment
Treatment starts immediately with no upfront payment
Police verification happens in parallel and does not need to be arranged by the victim
whereToApply: Any hospital empanelled under the PM-RAHAT scheme. Call 112 (Ambulance & Police) to locate the nearest one.
notes: Even private hospitals must provide initial treatment. If a hospital refuses treatment, report it to the district authority or call 14555 (hospital refusal helpline). For free legal help at any stage, call the NALSA helpline on 15100.

name: Hit and Run Compensation Scheme
description: Financial relief for victims or legal heirs when the vehicle that caused the accident fled the scene and could not be identified, even after police investigation.
eligibility: The driver or vehicle fled the scene
The vehicle was not identified even after police investigation
If the vehicle is identified later, the case moves to Section 164 (no-fault) or Section 166 (fault-based) instead, where compensation amounts are higher
benefits: ₹50,000 for grievous injuries (severe hurt as legally defined)
₹2,00,000 in case of death, claimable by the legal heir
Also applicable to the legal heir of a person who sustained grievous injuries and later died of a cause unrelated to the accident
process: Police must inform the victim/family about this scheme and transfer the file to the Claims Enquiry Officer / Sub Divisional Officer
Visit the Claims Enquiry Officer (CEO) with a copy of the FIR, post-mortem/injury report, death certificate, bank passbook, and ID proof
Submit Form I
The file is stamped 'Processing' and a case is opened for compensation
whereToApply: Claims Enquiry Officer / Sub Divisional Officer (SDO) notified for the district, following transfer of the file by the police.
notes: You can use more than one compensation door if applicable — Hit & Run, Section 164 (no-fault), and Section 166 (fault-based) are separate pathways. For free legal help at any stage, call NALSA on 15100.

name: Section 164 — No-Fault Compensation
description: Fixed, immediate relief compensation that does not require proving who caused the accident. Filed directly with the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT).
eligibility: Applies when the vehicle involved is known but negligence/fault is not clearly established
No need to prove who caused the accident
benefits: ₹2,50,000 for grievous injury
₹5,00,000 in case of death
Faster to settle than a fault-based claim, giving quick relief without a long court fight
Does not prevent applying later under Section 166 for higher compensation once fault or fuller losses are established
documents: Dependency proof (for dependents claiming after a death)
process: File a claim directly with the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT)
Keep FIR, death/disability certificate, Aadhaar, birth proof, and dependency proof ready — a lawyer or DLSA paralegal can help gather these for free
After filing, you receive a case number and the tribunal notifies the insurer and schedules a hearing
Section 164 claims are generally faster to settle than Section 166 claims
whereToApply: Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in the concerned district.
notes: This is immediate relief support. For free legal help with filing, call NALSA on 15100 or contact your District Legal Services Authority (DLSA).

name: Section 166 — Fault-Based Compensation
description: Full compensation claim for cases where the crash was clearly caused by someone else's negligence, such as drunk driving, overspeeding, or wrong-side driving.
eligibility: The crash was clearly someone else's fault — for example drunk driving, overspeeding, or wrong-side driving
Best suited to serious injury cases or loss of the family's main earner, where negligence is clear and losses are long-term
benefits: Covers the full picture of losses: medical costs, lost income, and emotional suffering
Can account for future care needs, not just immediate costs
Generally results in higher compensation than Section 164, but takes longer to settle
documents: Death certificate or disability certificate, as applicable
Dependency proof, medical bills, and income proof to support the full claim
process: File a fault-based claim petition before the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT)
Present evidence establishing the other party's negligence (e.g. drunk driving, overspeeding)
Support the claim with medical bills, income loss evidence, and future care cost estimates
Attend hearings; the tribunal decides the final compensation award
whereToApply: Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in the concerned district.
notes: Section 166 takes longer to settle than Section 164 but covers all losses. You can pursue Section 164 first for quick relief, then Section 166 for the fuller amount. For free legal help, call NALSA on 15100 or contact your DLSA.

name: Legal Aid through District Legal Services Authority
description: Free or subsidised legal assistance for eligible individuals to help them understand and pursue accident compensation claims.
eligibility: Individuals from economically weaker sections, women, children, and other eligible categories as defined under legal services rules
Persons unable to independently pursue a compensation or legal claim
benefits: Free legal advice and representation for eligible applicants
Assistance with drafting and filing claim petitions
Guidance on court procedures and documentation
documents: Any existing correspondence related to the case
process: Visit or contact the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) office
Explain the case and submit eligibility documents
Receive guidance or assigned legal representation, if eligible
whereToApply: District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) office in the concerned district, or the State Legal Services Authority helpline.
notes: Eligibility criteria and services vary by state. Users should confirm current eligibility norms with their local DLSA.

name: Disability Certificate Support
description: Guidance on obtaining an official disability certificate for accident survivors with long-term physical impact, which is often required for compensation and rehabilitation benefits.
eligibility: Accident survivor with a permanent or long-term physical impairment
Assessment by an authorised medical board
benefits: Official recognition of disability for compensation and benefit purposes
Access to disability-related welfare schemes and concessions
Supporting document for rehabilitation and employment support programs
documents: Referral letter from treating hospital, if available
process: Consult the treating hospital for a referral to the medical board
Attend assessment at the designated government hospital or medical board
Receive the disability certificate after evaluation
Use the certificate to apply for related benefits and support
whereToApply: District government hospital or medical board authorised to issue disability certificates.
notes: Assessment criteria and certificate formats can vary by state. Confirm the nearest authorised medical board with local health authorities.

name: Rehabilitation Support for Accident Survivors
description: Guidance on physical, psychological, and vocational rehabilitation support available to accident survivors during recovery.
eligibility: Accident survivors requiring physical therapy, psychological support, or vocational retraining
Open to all survivors regardless of case status
benefits: Access to physiotherapy and rehabilitation guidance
Psychological and emotional support resources
Vocational training and reintegration support through NGOs and community programs
process: Consult the treating hospital's rehabilitation department, if available
Reach out to local NGOs or road safety organisations for support programs
Enrol in physiotherapy, counselling, or vocational programs as recommended
whereToApply: Hospital rehabilitation departments, state health department programs, or local NGOs working in road safety and disability support.
notes: Availability of rehabilitation programs varies by location. Verify current programs with local health authorities or NGOs.

name: Emergency Medical Documentation Guidance
description: Guidance on the medical documentation typically required immediately after a road accident, which forms the basis for later compensation and legal claims.
eligibility: Applicable to all accident victims receiving emergency medical treatment
benefits: Clear understanding of documents needed at the time of treatment
Reduced difficulty in later compensation and legal processes
process: Ensure the victim is admitted and treated at a recognised hospital
Request copies of all medical records and bills at the time of treatment or discharge
Preserve original documents safely for future reference
whereToApply: Treating hospital's medical records or billing department.
notes: Hospitals may have specific procedures for issuing certified copies. Confirm requirements directly with the treating hospital.

name: Legal Heir Documentation for Death Cases
description: Guidance on obtaining legal heir certificates and related documents required when a road accident results in the death of a family member.
eligibility: Family members of a deceased accident victim seeking compensation or benefit transfer
benefits: Legal recognition of heirs for compensation, insurance, and benefit claims
Simplifies bank account and asset-related processes
documents: Identity proof of applicant and deceased
Family/relationship proof (ration card, family register, etc.)
process: Obtain the death certificate from the municipal or local body
Apply for a legal heir certificate at the local revenue or tehsil office
Use the certificate for compensation, insurance, and bank-related claims
whereToApply: Local Tehsildar office, revenue department, or municipal authority.
notes: Process and required documents can vary by state. Confirm the exact procedure with the local revenue office.

name: Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Accident Insurance Scheme (Rajasthan)
description: Free accident insurance cover of up to ₹10 lakh for Rajasthan families already enrolled in the Chiranjeevi / Mukhyamantri Ayushman health insurance scheme, in case of accidental death or permanent total disability.
eligibility: Family must already be insured under Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana or the Rajasthan Government Health Scheme
Applies to accidental death, or permanent total loss of hands, legs, or eyes due to an accident
Claim must be filed within 60 days of the accident/death (extendable to 90 days with a valid reason)
benefits: Free accident insurance cover of up to ₹10 lakh per insured family
Amount is paid directly into the bank account of the head of the family, linked to Jan Aadhaar
Runs alongside central schemes like PM-RAHAT, Hit & Run, and Section 164/166 — not a replacement for them
documents: Proof of enrolment under Chiranjeevi / Rajasthan Government Health Scheme
Death certificate or disability assessment, as applicable
process: Any adult member of the insured family fills the claim form on the official online portal
Submit within 60 days of the accident (up to 90 days with a stated reason for delay)
A decision is communicated by SMS to the claimant's registered mobile number
Appeals can be filed online with the Director, State Insurance and Provident Fund Department, within 30 days of the decision
whereToApply: State Insurance and Provident Fund Department, Rajasthan (online claim portal).
notes: This is separate from central government schemes and is administered by the Rajasthan State Insurance and Provident Fund Department. Verify current portal links and forms directly with the department before applying.

name: Rajasthan Chief Minister's Relief Fund — Accident Assistance
description: Emergency relief fund assistance from the Rajasthan state government for families affected by accidental death or serious injury, alongside its role in natural disaster relief.
eligibility: Applies to families of those killed or seriously injured in an accident in Rajasthan
Assessed case-by-case by the Chief Minister's Relief Fund office
benefits: One-time financial assistance for accidental death or serious injury
Can be combined with central schemes (PM-RAHAT, Hit & Run, Section 164/166) and with MCDBY accident insurance, since it draws from a separate state relief fund
documents: Death certificate or medical certificate of injury
process: Visit cmrelief.rajasthan.gov.in and apply online, or apply through the local Collector/SDM office
Submit supporting documents
Track status through the portal or by contacting the Relief Fund office
whereToApply: Office of the Chief Minister's Relief Fund, Rajasthan (cmrelief.rajasthan.gov.in), or the local District Collector's office.
notes: Relief amounts are set administratively and can change; confirm the current amount directly with the Relief Fund office before relying on any figure quoted elsewhere. Contact: 0141-2227679, cmrf.cmo@rajasthan.gov.in.

name: Insurance Claim Documentation Guidance
description: Guidance on documentation typically needed to file a motor insurance claim following a road accident.
eligibility: Vehicle involved in the accident has a valid insurance policy
Claim is intimated to the insurer within the specified timeframe
benefits: Structured guidance to help file a smoother insurance claim
Reduced chances of claim delay due to missing documents
documents: Driving licence and RC of the vehicle
Repair estimate or medical bills, as applicable
Photographs of the accident and damage
process: Inform the insurance company about the accident as soon as possible
Register a claim with the required documents
Cooperate with the surveyor or claims officer during assessment
Track claim status with the insurer
whereToApply: Concerned motor insurance company or its nearest branch/office.
notes: Claim procedures and timelines differ by insurer. Confirm specific requirements with the concerned insurance company.

name: Good Samaritan Awareness Resource
description: Awareness resource explaining the legal protections available to bystanders who help accident victims, encouraging timely assistance without fear of harassment.
eligibility: Applicable to any bystander or witness assisting an accident victim in good faith
benefits: Legal protection from unnecessary questioning or procedural harassment for good-faith helpers
Encourages faster emergency response and higher survival chances
process: If safe to do so, call emergency services or the nearest hospital immediately
Assist the victim to reach medical care without waiting for police formalities
Provide basic information to authorities if asked, in good faith
whereToApply: Not applicable — this is an awareness resource, not an application-based scheme.
notes: Good Samaritan protections and their exact scope are defined under applicable guidelines and law. This summary is for awareness only and does not constitute legal advice.
